purpose.summary: This copybook defines the data structure for PADFLPCB, which appears to be related to IMS PCB (Program Communication Block) information. It contains fields for DBD name, segment level, PCB status, processing options, segment name, key feedback name, number of sensitive segments, and key feedback area.

paragraphs.purpose: This copybook defines the structure of the PADFLPCB data area, which is likely used to hold Program Communication Block (PCB) information within an IMS (Information Management System) environment. The structure includes fields such as PADFL-DBDNAME (Database Description Name) to identify the database being accessed [18], PADFL-SEG-LEVEL (Segment Level) indicating the hierarchical level of the segment [19], PADFL-PCB-STATUS (PCB Status) providing status codes related to the PCB [20], and PADFL-PCB-PROCOPT (PCB Processing Options) specifying the processing options used for database access [21]. It also contains PADFL-SEG-NAME (Segment Name) to identify the segment being processed [23], PADFL-KEYFB-NAME (Key Feedback Name) and PADFL-NUM-SENSEGS (Number of Sensitive Segments) which likely relate to key feedback information and the number of sensitive segments defined in the database [24, 25]. Finally, PADFL-KEYFB (Key Feedback) provides a 255-byte area for key feedback data [26]. This copybook is intended to be included in COBOL programs that interact with an IMS database, allowing the program to access and manipulate PCB information.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Record layout 'PADFLPCB' is never used by any program
